lines.text: Ms. Patel reads names off a clipboard while the room hums with side conversations and the smell of dry-erase marker.
Trios. No trading, no negotiating — I've seen what happens when I allow negotiating.
Group four: Jordan Alvarez, Maya Okafor, Nico Salgado.
Yes! Okay — okay, let's go, team.
lines.action: snaps his fingers like he's already had an idea

lines.text: Hi.
lines.action: already squaring a stack of color-coded notecards against her desk

lines.text: Hey. So — history fair, huh. Cool. Very normal amount of pressure for a Tuesday.
New group. New group dynamics. Great. Love that for me.
Across the room, Casey catches Nico's eye and mouths something.
Good luck.
lines.action: mouths it silently, then adds out loud

lines.text: You're gonna need it — Jordan talks fast and Maya alphabetizes her pencils.
They're organized by hue, actually.
lines.action: doesn't miss a beat

lines.text: Find your groups, please. Historically speaking — you'll want the full three days.
Library study room. Jordan drops his backpack on the table like he's announcing something.
Okay okay, hear me out — what if the whole board is just, like, one big timeline, but it *pops*, you know?
lines.action: already talking with his hands

lines.text: Pops like... confetti? Or pops like—
Pops like — colors, layers, a *centerpiece*. I'm thinking 3D. I'm thinking people walk by and go 'whoa.'
I actually found some primary sources that could—
lines.action: opens her folder carefully

lines.text: Right, right, sources, yeah, we'll drop those in wherever. I'm just saying the *shape* of it matters first.
lines.action: already moving on, doesn't look at her folder

lines.text: ...Okay.
lines.action: closes the folder halfway, pulls it a little closer to her chest

lines.text: Jordan keeps talking, already sketching boxes on a napkin. Maya goes quiet — the kind of quiet that isn't agreement.
She had a whole thing to say. He just... didn't hear it. Do I say something or is that making it weird.
[CHOICE POINT: choice-1]
Jordan's kitchen table. Two days left. Laptops open, one half-finished poster board leaning against a chair.
Okay so here's the thing.
lines.action: spins his laptop around

lines.text: My brother did this exact fair, like, three years ago. Same rubric basically. I've got the whole template.
Wait, like — his actual project? From three years ago?
It's basically done, I just gotta finesse a couple things. Don't stress it. We swap some facts, boom, done by tonight.
lines.action: snaps his fingers

lines.text: That's — his project. Not ours.
lines.action: taps her pencil twice

lines.text: I mean, it's just a *structure*. Nobody's gonna know. And we're out of time, so.
lines.action: leans back, arms crossed

lines.text: Honestly? Two days is tight, but—
I already cross-referenced two of my sources. One of them's actually wrong, if it matters.
See, that's great, that's — we can just fit that in wherever. It's fine. It's basically the same amount of work either way, right?
[CHOICE POINT: choice-2]
Later. The template's closed. Highlighters everywhere. It's slower going, and it shows in everyone's shoulders.
This is gonna take forever.
It's actually kind of interesting once you get into it. This letter here — the guy's handwriting is a disaster, but the argument's solid.
lines.emotion: genuine, a little surprised at her own enthusiasm

lines.text: Wait, read that part again?
lines.action: leans over, actually looking now

lines.text: 'We hold these truths to be self-evident, except when inconvenient' — I'm paraphrasing, but not by a lot.
Okay that's — okay, that's actually kind of funny. Put that in.
See, this is way better than a stolen PowerPoint.
Don't say 'stolen,' it's — okay, fine. Yeah. This is better.
It's midnight and I'm having fun doing homework. I don't know what that says about me.
lines.action: small, real smile

lines.text: It says you're built different, honestly.
The template's open. It's fast — fonts already picked, layout already done. They're just swapping in facts.
See? This is the move. Barely even had to think.
Where do you want this source?
lines.action: typing without looking up

lines.text: Uh, slide four's fine. Just — wherever it fits.
lines.action: not really looking either

lines.text: Feels weird how fast this is going.
That's called *efficient*, my friend.
lines.action: high-fives the player without waiting for a response

lines.text: See? Nailed it. Group effort, baby.
...Right? Group effort.
Mm-hm.
lines.emotion: not agreeing, not fighting it either
lines.action: keeps typing

lines.text: This should feel like a win. It doesn't, quite.
The template's open, but so is Maya's folder — and every claim on the slide gets a little sticky-note flag before it's allowed to stay.
This is taking so long for something that's supposed to save time.
We just gotta cite where it actually came from. Ten more minutes.
This claim on slide six is unsourced. I can find something, give me a second.
lines.action: taps her pencil twice before writing

lines.text: It's a *template*, Maya, it's not supposed to be a whole research paper.
It doesn't have to be. It just has to be true.
...Fine. Fine, find your source.
lines.action: arms crossed, but he waits

lines.text: This is slower, but it's kind of starting to feel like ours, at least.
It better be worth it.
lines.emotion: grumbling, though he's still here at eleven at night

lines.text: It will be.
Library study room. Jordan's at the printer down the hall. Maya squares her notecards against the table edge, not quite meeting Nico's eyes.
Can I say something? It's not a big deal.
Yeah, of course. What's up?
I organized the timeline, the sources, the citations — most of the actual content, if I'm being honest.
And I don't know if my name's even going to be on the final slide. Jordan built that one.
lines.emotion: clipped, hurt trying not to show

lines.text: Wait, seriously? He didn't put your name on it?
I'm not trying to make it a whole thing. I just did most of it, and I guess I wanted someone to know that.
lines.emotion: quiet, this is the real ask

lines.text: It's fine either way. I'll just fix the formatting later if I have to.
lines.action: starts squaring the notecards again, a nervous ritual

lines.text: It's not fine, though. Not if you did the work.
No, it's — it's good, actually, the project. It just would've been nice if my name was on it too. That's all.
lines.emotion: trying to sound like it's smaller than it is

lines.text: [CHOICE POINT: choice-3]
Jordan's back from the printer, warm paper in hand, not expecting an ambush.
Hey, quick thing — the credits slide only has your name and mine on it.
I mean — it's not like I said it wasn't a group thing. I just... built the slide first. That's not a crime.
lines.action: arms crossed, a wall going up fast

lines.text: I organized every source in this project. I'd like my name on it.
lines.emotion: steady, surprising herself with how steady

lines.text: ...Yeah. No, you're right. That was — yeah. My bad.
lines.action: the arms uncross

lines.text: I'll fix it. Like, right now, actually.
lines.action: already pulling the laptop back over

lines.text: Thank you.
See? Wasn't that hard.
Don't push it.
I'm sure it'll be fine. Ms. Patel's usually pretty good about noticing who did what.
Sure.
Try not to stress about it, okay?
I won't.
lines.action: goes back to formatting slides, small and quiet

lines.text: That felt like the easy answer. I'm not totally sure it was the right one.
Anyway. I'll just — fix the formatting.
After school. Ms. Patel's classroom is empty except for the hum of the radiator. Nico lingers by the door.
Ms. Patel? Can I talk to you for a sec? It's about the project.
Of course.
lines.action: takes off her glasses to clean them on her cardigan hem

lines.text: I'm not asking to catch anyone. I'm asking because I'd actually like to know.
Honestly? We almost used a template from Jordan's brother's old project. We didn't, in the end, but it was close.
And Maya's done most of the actual research, but it wasn't going to have her name on it. I don't know if it still won't.
I appreciate that. That's not a small thing to bring to me before it's even a problem.
I'll be paying attention on presentation day. You don't have to do anything else.
Okay. Yeah. Thanks.
Go on, you'll be late for the bus.
Front of the classroom. The poster board's up, the slides are cued. Jordan's already talking before Ms. Patel finishes introducing them.
So this whole project actually started with an idea I had — the timeline concept, the layout, all of it.
And then obviously I did a ton of the research to back it up.
lines.action: clicks past a slide that's clearly all Maya's citations, barely glancing at her

lines.text: Maya's hands go still on her notecards. She doesn't say anything.
From the back row, Casey catches Nico's eye — a small, deliberate look.
[CHOICE POINT: choice-4]
Actually — hang on. Maya did the research on this slide. Like, all of it.
Two of the sources, actually. But yes.
lines.emotion: dry, but her shoulders drop with relief

lines.text: Right — sorry. Maya did the research. I just built the slides around it.
Good to know.
lines.action: taps her pen against her mug, a small nod

lines.text: Thank you.
lines.emotion: quiet, to Jordan, and maybe to the player too

lines.text: The presentation wraps. A scattering of applause. Jordan does a small, easy bow.
Thank you, thank you — group effort, really.
lines.emotion: performing modesty he doesn't quite mean

lines.text: Maya says nothing. She gathers her notecards, edges square, and walks back to her seat without meeting anyone's eyes.
Nice work, all three of you.
lines.emotion: even, though her eyes linger on Maya a beat longer than necessary

lines.text: After the bell. Ms. Patel's classroom empties out except for Nico, hovering near her desk.
How did it really go?
lines.action: takes off her glasses, cleans them, waits

lines.text: It went—
lines.emotion: trails off, unsure which version of the truth he's about to tell

lines.text: Take your time.
lines.action: three full seconds of silence, the kind she's known for

lines.text: [CHOICE POINT: choice-5]
We almost used Jordan's brother's old project as a shortcut. And Maya did most of the actual work, but it almost didn't have her name on it.
I appreciate that. I mean that every time I say it, but especially now.
I'll grade with full information. That's all that means — full information.
Even if it costs us a little on the grade?
Even then. Though I'd rather reward the fixing than punish the mistake, historically speaking.
Historically speaking?
Don't push it. Go on, you'll be late for lunch.
It had some rough patches. We figured most of it out, though.
Rough patches.
lines.action: taps her pen against her mug, waits three seconds
lines.emotion: not quite buying it, not quite pushing either

lines.text: Yeah. Nothing major.
Okay.
lines.emotion: lets it sit there a beat too long to be fully comfortable

lines.text: If anything ever needs revisiting, my door's open. Even after grades are in.
lines.emotion: leaving the door open on purpose

lines.text: Got it. Thanks.
lines.emotion: relief that's a little too quick

lines.text: It went great, honestly. Everyone contributed equally.
Good to hear.
lines.action: beat, three full seconds

lines.text: Go on, you'll be late for lunch.
That should've felt like getting away with something. Mostly it just feels like a rock in my stomach.
Cafeteria. Casey slides into the seat across from Nico with a tray and a look that already knows something.
So. You gonna tell me how it actually went, or should I just watch your face while you lie to me?
Wow. Rude. Accurate, but rude.
Hm.
lines.action: tilts head, squints, waits

options.text: Wait — I think Maya had something. Let's actually hear it.
What if we combined both ideas?

a.emotion: slightly caught off guard, recovers fast

a.action: sits up a little, folder loosening in her grip

a.text: I found some letters from actual people who lived through it — not just textbook summaries. It could ground the whole board in something real.
Huh. Okay, yeah, that's — that could work with the timeline thing.

b.text: Nico says nothing. Jordan keeps sketching his boxes. Maya's folder stays pulled tight against her chest.
It's fine. I'll just... I'll fit the sources in wherever there's room.
That felt like the easy way out. I'm not sure it actually was easy.

c.text: What if we combined both ideas — your layout, but built around Maya's sources?
Oh, see, that's — yeah, okay, I can work with that.

c.action: loosens her grip on the folder just slightly

options.text: No — let's just do the real work. I'll help.
Fine, just this once. It's not a big deal.
Let's use it as a structure, but cite everything properly.

a.text: Wait, seriously? We've got two days.
a.emotion: half-relieved someone else is committing to the harder thing

a.text: Two days is enough if we all actually work instead of finessing a shortcut.
I already have half the sources organized. This is doable.

a.action: closes the laptop with the template

b.text: It's just this once. That's what I keep telling myself, anyway.

c.text: Let's use the structure, but every fact gets a real source. No exceptions.
That's gonna take way longer than 'just swap some facts.'
It'll take exactly as long as it needs to.
c.action: already pulling her folder back open

options.text: That's not okay. Let's fix it with Jordan, together.
I'm sure it'll work out — try not to worry.
I think we should talk to Ms. Patel about how this actually came together.

a.text: You'd actually say something? To him?
Yeah. It's not a big deal for me to bring up. It clearly is one for you.

a.emotion: quiet, means it more than the word carries

b.action: goes back to her notecards, smaller than before

b.text: I said the comforting thing. I'm not sure I said the true thing.

c.text: Ms. Patel? I don't want to make it a whole—
Not to get anyone in trouble. Just so there's a record of what actually happened, in case it matters later.

options.text: Actually, Maya did most of this research.
(Catch Jordan's eye. Gesture him toward crediting Maya.)

a.text: Actually — Maya did most of this research. Like, all the sourcing on the board is hers.
a.action: the room goes a little quiet
goes very still for a second

a.text: Good to know. Go on, Jordan.

b.text: Nico says nothing. Jordan finishes strong. The class claps.
That's a thing I didn't do. I'll be thinking about that for a while.

c.text: Nico catches Jordan's eye mid-sentence and tilts his head toward Maya, just slightly.
— and, uh, actually, a lot of this came from Maya's research. Like, most of it.
c.emotion: recovering smoothly, but it's clearly not his original plan

c.text: Two of the sources are wrong, if anyone wants to know which ones.

options.text: Tell her everything — the shortcut, the credit issue, all of it.
Mention 'some rough patches' without naming names.
It went great, everyone contributed equally.

a.text: Honestly? Not perfectly. Can I just tell you the whole thing?

b.text: There were some rough patches. We worked through most of it.

b.emotion: noting the word choice, not challenging it yet

c.text: It went great, honestly. Everyone contributed equally.
c.emotion: too smooth, and he knows it

lines.text: I told Ms. Patel everything. The shortcut we almost used, the credit thing with Maya — all of it.
Huh. Didn't peg you for the 'tell the teacher everything' type.
Yeah, well. Maya actually looked at me today without that whole 'bracing for disappointment' thing. Feels worth it.
Noticing counts for something. Turns out saying something counts for more, huh.
It went fine. Nobody yelled, nobody cried, credits got fixed. Jordan doesn't even know I engineered half of it.
So the group's better and Jordan thinks it just... happened.
Sometimes the quiet way works. You just gotta actually do something with the quiet.
Look at you. Diplomat era.
It went fine, I guess. Nothing bad happened.
Hm. That's not the same as 'good.'
Maya barely talked to me on the walk out. I don't know. I keep thinking there was a moment I should've said something and didn't.
Noticing counts for something. It just counts for less than saying something. Told you that once already.
I told Patel it went great. Everyone contributed equally.
So. You gonna tell me how it actually went, or should I just watch your face while you lie to me?
lines.emotion: gentle, but not letting him off easy

lines.text: ...We used a shortcut. Jordan took the credit. Maya hasn't really looked at me since.
Yeah. Your face already told me that part.
lines.emotion: not gloating, just tired for him

lines.text: I actually went to Patel before presentation day. Told her about the shortcut and the credit thing before it even played out in front of everyone.
Huh. So there was no big blow-up in front of the class?
lines.emotion: surprised, a little disappointed for the drama

lines.text: Nope. Just a very calm, very long conversation about 'full information.' Somehow scarier than the blow-up would've been.
That tracks. She's got a whole PhD in calm.

voiceNotes: Jordan front-loads confidence and rarely finishes explaining a joke that already landed — his defensiveness ('it's not like I said it wasn't a group thing') is a tell that he knows he's wrong before he admits it. Maya undersells everything ('it's not a big deal') until pushed, then gets precise and clipped rather than emotional — her hurt shows in shortness, not volume. Nico's sarcasm ('Very normal amount of pressure for a Tuesday') is a pressure valve; his 'honestly' tic should appear right before choice-adjacent lines where he's not fully sure of himself. Casey never asks a direct emotional question twice — the 'hm' does the work silence should do. Ms. Patel's three-second pauses should be represented as a beat/action tag rather than described outright, letting the player feel the weight of it rather than being told about it.

alternatives: She had a whole thing to say. He just talked right over it. Is that a me problem now?
Okay that was a little rough. Do I say something or let it go?
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeps Nico's internal hedging pattern ('Do I say something or is that making it weird') while offering a slightly more casual register if the tone needs to skew lighter early in the episode.

alternatives: Good to hear.' [beat] 'Go on, you'll be late for lunch.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Matches Ms. Patel's canonical voice example almost verbatim for the complicit path, reinforcing that her calm response is a known pattern, not a one-off — making the player's guilt land harder against her unreadable consistency.
